sections.paragraph:
an unused root.-

(1) to bend, to inflect (Arab. آدَ med. Waw); hence, to turn, to turn about, to turn over, see subst. אוּד , אֹודֹות ; also to gird, to surround, see אֵד . With this agree עוּד and Eth. አዊድ፡ [this last is omitted in Amer. Trans.]. Hence-

(2) to load, to press down, as a burden, whence آيِدُ heavy, troublesome; أَوْدَةُ a load, a weight; مَأَاوِدُ misfortune, ills, with which any one is pressed down; see אֵיד .

(3) i.q. أَادَ for أَيَدَ to be strong, robust, Conj. II. to strengthen, to aid; أَيْدُ and آدُ strength, might, power, whence the Hebrew מְאֹד; comp. קָשָׁה and other verbs which have the notions of weight and strength conjoined.
